test_cases.name: TC-1: Default grid load — verify columns and pagination [Priority: High]
test_cases.test_data: Preconditions: User logged in as Store Rep, storeType = COR; UAT environment is up; RTPL-1818 BE is deployed and returning data. | Test Data: role = Store Rep, storeType = COR, URL = /transactions/listing

steps.action: Navigate to Transactions
Wait for the data grid to fully load (loading spinner disappears).
Inspect the column headers visible in the grid.
Verify the pager shows page size selector and navigation controls.
steps.expected: Grid renders with 7 default columns: Invoice Number, Date, Amount Paid, Account #, Account Name, Employee ID, Station ID. Rows are populated and paginated at 10 per page. Amount Paid values display as formatted currency (not raw numbers or $0.00 for non-null values).

test_cases.name: TC-2: Keyword search — by invoice number and by employee ID [Priority: High]
test_cases.test_data: Preconditions: Grid is loaded with at least 2 records from different employees. A known saleNumber and a known employeeId are available in test data. | Test Data: saleNumber = known invoice number from UAT dataset, employeeId = known employee ID from UAT dataset

steps.action: Type the known invoice number into the keyword search field and wait for the grid to filter.
Confirm the grid shows only the matching record(s), then clear the search field.
Type the known employee ID into the keyword search field and wait for the grid to filter.
Confirm the grid shows only records associated with that employee.
steps.expected: Each keyword search correctly narrows the grid to matching records. Clearing the search restores the full unfiltered result set. Search operates against saleNumber and employeeId fields only (not accountName or other fields).

test_cases.name: TC-3: Date range filter with Apply — valid range and empty range [Priority: High]
test_cases.test_data: Preconditions: Grid is loaded; test data has records spanning multiple calendar dates. A date range with no records is known (e.g. a historical range with no transactions). | Test Data: dateFrom = 2026-05-01, dateTo = 2026-05-10; emptyRange = 2000-01-01 to 2000-01-02

steps.action: Click the Date filter control in the toolbar.
Select start date 2026-05-01 and end date 2026-05-10, then click Apply.
Verify all visible saleDate values fall within the selected range and confirm the pager total updates.
Change the date range to 2000-01-01 through 2000-01-02 and click Apply.
Observe the grid content area.
steps.expected: After step 3, only in-range records appear and the pager count reflects the filtered set. After step 5, an empty state is displayed with no crash or infinite spinner.

test_cases.name: TC-4: Sale type filter — All / Sale / Payment [Priority: High]
test_cases.test_data: Preconditions: Grid is loaded; test data includes records with saleType = "Sale" and saleType = "Payment". | Test Data: Filter By dropdown options = All, Sale, Payment; test records contain both types

steps.action: Confirm the Filter By dropdown defaults to showing all record types.
Select "Sale" from the dropdown and observe the grid results.
Select "Payment" from the dropdown and observe the grid results.
Select "All" from the dropdown and observe the grid results.
steps.expected: Selecting "Sale" shows only records where saleType = Sale. Selecting "Payment" shows only records where saleType = Payment. Selecting "All" removes the type filter and all records return. Each selection automatically refreshes the grid without requiring a manual reload.

test_cases.name: TC-5: Combined filter — date range AND sale type [Priority: Medium]
test_cases.test_data: Preconditions: Grid is loaded; test data has both Sale and Payment records within a known date range. | Test Data: dateFrom = 2026-05-01, dateTo = 2026-05-15, saleType = Payment; expected result: only Payment records within that window

steps.action: Apply the date range filter (2026-05-01 to 2026-05-15) and click Apply.
Set the sale type dropdown to "Payment".
Inspect the grid rows for saleDate and saleType values.
steps.expected: The grid shows only records where saleDate is within the range AND saleType = Payment. No records outside the date window or with saleType = Sale appear. The combined filter does not cause a UI error or reset either filter.

test_cases.name: TC-6: Optional column toggle — add and remove columns [Priority: Medium]
test_cases.test_data: Preconditions: Grid is loaded with the 7 default columns visible. | Test Data: Optional toggleable columns: Sale Type (dataField = saleType), Wholesale (dataField = isWholesale)

steps.action: Click the Columns multiselect button in the grid toolbar.
Toggle on the "Sale Type" column and the "Wholesale" column.
Confirm both new columns appear in the grid with correct data.
Toggle both columns off via the multiselect.
Confirm the grid returns to the 7 default columns with no layout errors.
steps.expected: Sale Type and Wholesale columns appear and display correct values (saleType string and isWholesale boolean respectively) when toggled on. Toggling off removes them cleanly. The 7 default columns remain intact throughout.
